title: Undercut And Field-Pressure Signals
summary: F1 pit timing is strategic, not purely tyre wear: undercut, traffic, relative position, and pit-lane loss determine whether a stop is attractive.
rationale: F1 pit timing is strategic, not purely tyre wear: undercut, traffic, relative position, and pit-lane loss determine whether a stop is attractive. Race-strategy references explicitly model traffic and undercut conditions.
implementation_hint: Create same-lap field aggregates by Year/Race/LapNumber: driver tyre-life percentile, position-neighborhood tyre-life mean, count/share of cars with older tyres, median lap delta by position band, and whether nearby positions have worse pace/older tyres. Add interactions with Position, Position_Change, RaceProgress, and Estimated_Laps_Remaining.
expected_effect: Could improve discrimination among drivers with similar tyre age where only track-position context makes a pit likely.
risk: No explicit gap/safety-car data is available, so proxies may be noisy; aggregate features must be computed identically on concatenated train/test without target labels.
